Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal

Class StringValidatorImpl

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension.AbstractObjectValidator<StringValidator,String>
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.StringValidatorImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
ExtensibleObjectValidator<StringValidator,String>, StringValidator
public final class StringValidatorImpl extends AbstractObjectValidator<StringValidator,String> implements StringValidator
Default implementation of StringValidator.

    Constructor
    Description
    StringValidatorImpl(ApplicationScope scope, Configuration config, String name, String actual, List<ValidationFailure> failures, boolean fatalFailure)
    Creates a StringValidatorImpl with existing validation failures.

    Ensures that the actual value contains a value.
    StringValidator
    doesNotContain(String value)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain a value.
    StringValidator

    Ensures that the actual value does not end with a value.
    StringValidator
    doesNotStartWith(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value does not start with a value.
    StringValidator
    endsWith(String suffix)
    Ensures that the actual value ends with a value.
    StringValidator
    isBlank()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty or contains only white space codepoints.
    StringValidator
    isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    StringValidator
    isNotBlank()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty or contains non-white space codepoints.
    StringValidator
    isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    StringValidator
    isStripped()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by StringValidator.strip().
    StringValidator
    isTrimmed()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by StringValidator.trim().
    SizeValidator
    length()
    Returns a validator for the length of the actual value.
    StringValidator
    length(Consumer<SizeValidator> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    StringValidator
    startsWith(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value starts with a value.
    StringValidator
    strip()
    Strips white space from the beginning and the end of the actual value, where white space is defined by the Unicode standard.
    StringValidator
    trim()
    Removes all white space from the beginning and end of the actual value, where white space is defined as any character whose codepoint is less than or equal to 'U+0020' (the space character).

    asString, getActual, getFailures, isEqualTo, isEqualTo, isInstanceOf, isNotEqualTo, isNotEqualTo, isNotInstanceOf, isNotNull, isNotOneOf, isNotSameObjectAs, isNull, isOneOf, isSameObjectAs

  • Constructor Details

    • StringValidatorImpl

      public StringValidatorImpl(ApplicationScope scope, Configuration config, String name, String actual, List<ValidationFailure> failures, boolean fatalFailure)
      Creates a StringValidatorImpl with existing validation failures.
      Parameters:
      scope - the application configuration
      config - the instance configuration
      name - the name of the value
      actual - the actual value
      failures - the list of validation failures
      fatalFailure - true if validation stopped as the result of a fatal failure
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if scope, config, name or failures are null. If name is blank.

      Description copied from interface: StringValidator

    • trim

      public StringValidator trim()
      Description copied from interface: StringValidator
      Removes all white space from the beginning and end of the actual value, where white space is defined as any character whose codepoint is less than or equal to 'U+0020' (the space character).
      Specified by:
      trim in interface StringValidator
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      See Also:
      • String.trim()

    • isTrimmed

      public StringValidator isTrimmed()
      Description copied from interface: StringValidator
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by StringValidator.trim().
      Specified by:
      isTrimmed in interface StringValidator
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      See Also:
      • StringValidator.trim()
      • StringValidator.isBlank()

      public StringValidator strip()
      Description copied from interface: StringValidator
      Strips white space from the beginning and the end of the actual value, where white space is defined by the Unicode standard.
      Specified by:
      strip in interface StringValidator
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      See Also:
      • String.strip()

      Ensures that the actual value contains a valid Internet address format.

      Returns:
      a validator for the Internet address representation of the value

    • asInetAddress

      public StringValidator asInetAddress(Consumer<InetAddressValidator> consumer)
      Description copied from interface: StringValidator
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See StringValidator.asInetAddress() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Specified by:
      asInetAddress in interface StringValidator
      Parameters:
      consumer - validates Internet addresses
      Returns:
      the updated validator

    • asUri

      public UriValidator asUri()
      Description copied from interface: StringValidator
      Returns a validator for the URI representation of the value.

      Technically-speaking, there is no such thing as an invalid URI format. Per RFC3986, any String can be represented as a relative URI, but Java's implementation is based on an older specification where this was not the case.

      Specified by:
      asUri in interface StringValidator
      Returns:
      a validator for the URI representation of the value
      See Also:
      • Discussion of Java URI vs RFC3986

    • asUri

      public StringValidator asUri(Consumer<UriValidator> consumer)
      Description copied from interface: StringValidator
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See StringValidator.asUri() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Specified by:
      asUri in interface StringValidator
      Parameters:
      consumer - validates URIs
      Returns:
      the updated validator
